name: Crush

form_factor.comment: Terminal-first AI coding agent with a full-screen Bubble Tea TUI plus a `crush run` CLI/headless mode. Per-form-factor links: CLI/TUI install via package managers or `go install`.

released_in.comment: First tagged release v0.1.0, committed 2025-07-29. Repo created 2025-05-21.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest tagged release v0.81.0 (2026-06-29) is a UX-focused release adding a chat scrollbar and other interface/session improvements.

rules.comment: Auto-loaded global/project context files: `~/.config/crush/CRUSH.md` (Crush-specific) and `~/.config/AGENTS.md`/project `AGENTS.md` (generic, cross-tool standard). Path override via `global_context_paths`; supports folder recursion for `.md` files.

skills.comment: Supports the open 'Agent Skills' standard (SKILL.md packages). Discoverable from `$CRUSH_SKILLS_DIR`, `~/.config/agents/skills/`, and project `.agents/skills/`; invocable via the command palette with `user:`/`project:` prefixes; toggle via `disabled_skills`/`skills_paths` config.

hooks.comment: "Crush has preliminary support for hooks" per the README, deferring to a separate hook guide. Early/limited lifecycle hook support compared to mature hook systems.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support across stdio, http, and sse transports, configured under `mcp` in crush.json. Supports shell-style value expansion ($VAR, ${VAR:-default}, $(command)), required-credential syntax (${VAR:?message}), per-server `disabled_tools`, and per-server timeouts.

custom_commands.comment: User-defined Markdown command files placed in `~/.config/crush/commands/` (or project-level), invoked from the command palette (press `/` or Ctrl+P, then Tab to list user commands). Public docs (COMMANDS.md) not yet merged to main as of mid-2026 but the feature is shipped.

goal_command.comment: No built-in `/goal` persistent-objective auto-continue loop. Sessions and `crush run --continue` support resuming work, but no dedicated goal-tracking/completion-condition command.

subagents.comment: Built-in "Coder" and "Task" agent types; the Coder agent can spawn restricted-permission Task subagents for delegated subtasks via an agent/task tool, with costs rolling up to the parent session. Agents configurable (model, allowed tools/MCP, context paths) via the `agents` config section.

monitor.comment: Crush can delegate to Task subagents and has preliminary hooks, but reviewed docs do not show a dedicated monitor/watch primitive for long-running commands or event streams.

transcripts.comment: Local SQLite-backed session storage with a dedicated `crush session` CLI subcommand: `list`, `show <id>`, `last`, `delete`, `rename`, each supporting `--json` for machine-readable transcripts including full message parts (text, reasoning, tool calls/results).

model_selection.comment: `-m/--model` and `--small-model` flags on `crush run`; in-TUI model switching mid-session while preserving context. Model database from Catwalk (community-supported open registry), auto-updated unless `disable_provider_auto_update`/`CRUSH_DISABLE_PROVIDER_AUTO_UPDATE=1` is set.

approval_mode.comment: Default mode prompts for permission before tool calls. `-y/--yolo` flag (or `permissions.yolo`/`SkipPermissionRequests`) auto-accepts all permissions. Granular `permissions.allowed_tools` allowlist for specific tools without prompting.

sandbox_mode.comment: No filesystem/network sandbox. Safety relies on permission prompts and the `--yolo` opt-out, plus `.crushignore` to exclude files from agent access.

resume.comment: `-s/--session <id>` resumes a specific prior session by full UUID, hash, or hash prefix; works both for the interactive TUI (`crush --session`) and `crush run --session`.

continue.comment: `-C/--continue` flag continues the most recently updated top-level session without needing an ID, for both `crush` (TUI) and `crush run` (non-interactive).

non_interactive.comment: `crush run "<prompt>"` (alias `r`) runs a single prompt non-interactively and exits; supports stdin piping, output redirection, `-q/--quiet` (hide spinner), `-v/--verbose` (show logs), and session continuation flags.

output_format.comment: `crush session list/show/last/delete/rename --json` give structured machine-readable output. `crush run` itself streams plain assistant text to stdout (no `--output-format json` for the run command).

statusline.comment: No user-configurable status line concept documented; the TUI shows fixed chrome (model, session, busy state) rather than a customizable status line.

telemetry.comment: Pseudonymous usage metrics tied to a device-specific hash; prompts/responses are never collected. Opt-out via `CRUSH_DISABLE_METRICS=1`, `disable_metrics: true` in config, or the `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` convention.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Built-in keybindings (e.g. `/` or Ctrl+P for command palette, Ctrl+O to open editor) exist and are documented in-app, but no general user-remappable keybindings config has been confirmed; some keybinding behavior is still being refined (open issues).

theme_customization.comment: Colors are auto-derived per model provider and dark/light terminal background detection (via charmtone/lipgloss); a TODO comment in config.go notes themes may be added later, but no user-selectable theme config exists yet. `transparent` TUI background toggle is supported.

auto_save.comment: Sessions/messages persist automatically to a local SQLite database under the data directory; no manual save action is required.

debugging_output.comment: Global `-d/--debug` flag and `debug`/`debug_lsp` config options; `crush run -v/--verbose` shows logs; `crush logs [--tail N] [--follow]` views/tails the log file at `./.crush/logs/crush.log`.

context_window.comment: No user-configurable context window/token-limit override documented; context window is determined by the selected model/provider via the Catwalk model database.

code_formatting.comment: No built-in formatter/linter integration documented beyond LSP-provided diagnostics; relies on LSP servers (gopls, typescript-language-server, etc.) for code intelligence rather than auto-formatting.

git_integration.comment: Respects `.gitignore` by default (plus `.crushignore` for extra exclusions); commit/PR message attribution customizable via `attribution.trailer_style` (assisted-by/co-authored-by/none) and `generated_with`. No dedicated diff-view UI or branch-management commands beyond what the agent does via shell tools.

performance_mode.comment: No documented caching/performance-tuning controls beyond standard provider model selection (large/small model split for cost/speed tradeoffs).

ui_customization.comment: TUI options include `compact_mode`, `diff_mode`, completion behavior, and `transparent` background toggle, but no panel/layout rearrangement or sidebar customization.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as a terminal binary (optionally with a local client/server mode via `crush serve` for shared workspaces); no Charm-hosted cloud agent platform.

custom_model_provider.comment: Connects to Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, Vercel AI Gateway, Google Gemini, Synthetic, Z.ai, MiniMax, Hugging Face, Cerebras, io.net, Alibaba, Avian, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Amazon Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, and local models (Ollama, LM Studio, MLX, LiteLLM) via Catwalk's open model registry plus fully custom OpenAI-/Anthropic-compatible provider configs.

notes: Crush is Charm's open-source (FSL-1.1-MIT), terminal-first AI coding agent, first released July 2025 and under very active daily development (180+ tagged releases by mid-2026, 25k+ GitHub stars). It pairs a polished Bubble Tea TUI with a scriptable `crush run` headless mode, broad MCP/LSP/provider support via the community Catwalk model registry, and an emerging Agent Skills + custom-commands + Task-subagent ecosystem. Notable gaps versus more mature agents: no sandboxing, no user-selectable themes yet (TODO in source), and only preliminary lifecycle hooks.

pricing.comment: Free and source-available under the Functional Source License (converts to MIT after 2 years per version). Users pay only for their own LLM provider usage; some providers offer Crush-specific coding plans (GLM Coding Plan, Kimi Code, MiniMax Coding Plan).

first_party_coding_model_plans.comment: No native first-party model subscription is documented for this surface in the current matrix source; model usage is BYOK, local, or provider-billed.

some_free_usage.comment: Local model backends (Ollama, LM Studio, MLX, LiteLLM) and some free-tier provider plans allow free agent usage without paying for hosted inference.

no_account_required.comment: Can run entirely against local Ollama/LM Studio/MLX models without any Crush account or vendor sign-in; the CLI itself requires no account to install or run.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Routes through the user's own API keys/accounts for any supported provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) or coding-plan subscriptions (GLM Coding Plan, Kimi Code, MiniMax Coding Plan, Synthetic) rather than a Crush-owned subscription.
